title: Why Tenable Stock Is Racing Higher Again Today
author: Scott Levine
description: Tenable stock surged 9.21% as JPMorgan analyst Brian Essex raised his price target from $35 to $40 and added the cybersecurity company to its Analyst Focus List. Essex cited escalating threats from China driving demand for Tenable's exposure management solutions. The stock also benefited from Tenable's recent FedRAMP High authorization from the U.S. government, making it eligible for federal cloud contracts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: JPMorgan raised price target by $5 (14% increase), added stock to Analyst Focus List with overweight rating, company achieved FedRAMP High authorization expanding federal contract eligibility, strong revenue and free cash flow growth, and trading at reasonable 16.6x forward earnings valuation.

title: American Express vs. SoFi Technologies: Which Financial Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Sara Appino
description: The article compares American Express (AXP), a premium legacy payments network with $233B market cap and 21.26 P/E ratio, against SoFi Technologies (SOFI), a rapidly growing fintech platform with $23B market cap and 41.26 P/E ratio. AXP posted $72.2B revenue (+10% YoY) with 15% net margin, while SOFI achieved $3.6B revenue (+38% YoY) with 13.4% net margin. The author recommends American Express for its proven track record, strong premium customer retention, and pricing power, while acknowledging SoFi's impressive growth but citing its relative youth and stock volatility as concerns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong financial performance with $72.2B revenue (+10% YoY), 15% net margin, excellent premium customer retention, accelerating card spending (fastest in 3 years), and proven business model with pricing power. Author explicitly recommends it as the better buy for 2026.

Impressive growth metrics with $3.6B revenue (+38% YoY), 13.4% net margin, and 14.7M members, but carries higher valuation risk (41.26 P/E), stock volatility, and is still proving itself through a full economic cycle. Author acknowledges potential but prefers established track record.

title: TrustEvals, Founded by Unmukt Raizada, Launches Next-Generation AI Governance for Financial Services, Real Estate firms, and Enterprise Audits
author: Not Specified
description: TrustEvals, founded by former Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase executive Unmukt Raizada, has launched an AI governance and audit platform designed to provide continuous evaluation and compliance monitoring for financial services, real estate, and enterprise sectors. The platform addresses vulnerabilities in AI deployment such as model drift and policy violations through real-time tracking rather than traditional point-in-time audits.

title: America's Biggest Banks Passed Their Stress Tests. Now They're Showering Investors With Cash.
author: Daniel Sparks
description: All 32 major U.S. banks passed the Federal Reserve's 2026 stress test, maintaining capital levels even under severe recession scenarios. JPMorgan Chase announced a $50 billion buyback and 10% dividend increase, while Morgan Stanley delivered the largest dividend increase at 15%. Goldman Sachs raised dividends 11%, and Wells Fargo increased dividends 11%. The announcements reflect strong confidence in bank stability, with Wells Fargo standing out as the cheapest valuation with the highest dividend yield.

title: Jefferies Reports Earnings Before the Big Banks. Here's Why Wall Street Should Be Watching Closely.
author: Dave Kovaleski
description: Jefferies Financial reported mixed Q2 earnings with net earnings and revenue missing estimates, but achieved record investment banking revenue of $1.2 billion (up 58% YoY). While asset management revenue declined 46% due to market weakness and Point Bonita losses, the strong investment banking results suggest upcoming quarters for Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan Chase should be robust, as they won't face the same asset management headwinds.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed results with earnings and revenue misses offset by record investment banking performance. Stock declined 6.72% on earnings release, but underlying business fundamentals in core investment banking are strong.

Jefferies' record investment banking results suggest strong Q2 performance ahead for Goldman Sachs. Additionally, Goldman won't face the same asset management headwinds as Jefferies since their quarter starts in April after market recovery.
